answer:
When a hologram is made with wavelength \(\lambda_1\) and viewed with a different wavelength \(\lambda_2\), the image distance scales by the ratio \(\lambda_2 / \lambda_1\). Here, \(\lambda_1 = 600\) nm and \(\lambda_2 = 500\) nm, so the ratio is \(500/600 = 5/6\). Thus, the reconstructed image distance becomes \(5/6\) of the original object distance.

\boxed{F}
